result:
Build is green. My eprintln has been reverted.

## Report

**Root cause:** This is not a fixable bug at the current phase — it's a Phase E gate. The test reaches line 187 (`assert(coroutine.wrap(function() return foo() end)() == 1023)` in `reference/lua-c/testes/calls.lua`) which genuinely requires coroutine execution. The error message originates from the deliberate Phase A–D stub at `crates/lua-stdlib/src/coro_lib.rs:113` (`aux_resume`), which per PORTING.md §2 #6 ("Coroutines stubbed in A–D") returns `-1` with the "coroutines not yet implemented (Phase E)" sentinel.

**Investigation:** I instrumented `aux_resume` with an eprintln to confirm the call site. The output showed it triggered exactly once — after the C-stack-overflow `xpcall(loop, loop)` test (line 165) successfully unwound through many `func::close` invocations and the test reached line 187. Every line 1–186 executed correctly; the test is fundamentally Phase-E-gated.

**Fix:** None made. The Phase A–D stub behavior is correct per the port strategy. I removed the temporary eprintln I added; no other code changes. `cargo build -q -p lua-cli` is green.

**New state:** Identical to before — the test still fails with the same coroutine-not-implemented error. The mega-loop's stuck-detect should classify this as `same sig 2 rounds` and skip it on the next pass (as already happened for `literals.lua`, which has the same signature).

tool_input.description: Run truncated test up to line 186
